title: Vernonanthura cordata
taxonomy: Vernonanthura cordata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America and belongs to the genus Vernonanthura. The genus Vernonanthura is part of the tribe Vernonieae, which includes a large number of flowering plants. Vernonanthura cordata is closely related to other species within the genus, such as Vernonanthura lanata and Vernonanthura fulgida. Vernonanthura cordata is a valuable part of its ecosystem, providing food and shelter for various animals and insects.

intro: Vernonanthura cordata, also known as Brazilian plume flower or Bracteantha, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Vernonanthura cordata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vernonanthura cordata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into creams and salves for topical application.
